rulings.Case Title: CLINTON BOGAN JR. et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION (SUED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 30, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant CBS Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY FREER et al VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN JOAQUIN COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant E.W. Bowman, Inc. To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 30, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant E.W. Bowman, Inc.¿s motion to transfer venue to the County of San Joaquin is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JACK SOWERS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Appoint Diane Sowers As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Pltf And For Leave To File 1st Amended Complt; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 30, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Plaintiffs¿ motion to appoint Diane Sowers as successor-in-interest to and substitute successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PERNIZ VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 30, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiff¿s complaint is granted with 15 days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity, if he is able to do so, to state facts sufficient to plead a claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff did not substantively oppose defendant¿s motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY CRAWFORD VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, MAY 30, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Grinnell LLC¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s first amended complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity, if she is able to do so, to demonstrate that her claim against defendant is not barred by the statute of limitations. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing.
